narration: Your bank just raised your credit limit and called it a reward. But this gift was designed to make you spend more — and the numbers prove it.
visual: Animated credit card SVG centered on dark background, credit limit counter animating from $5,000 to $8,500, green 'LIMIT INCREASED' badge, then red 'OR IS IT A TRAP?' warning fades in

narration: The second your limit jumps, your brain recalibrates. Psychologists call it the wealth effect — you feel richer, so you start spending like it.
visual: Brain SVG with folds on light background, dollar-sign thought bubble animates in, person silhouette below, 'THE WEALTH EFFECT' label slides up

narration: Here's the stat they bury: 68% of people spend up to their new limit within 18 months. Your brain fills the bucket every single time.
visual: Large '68%' counter animates from 0 to 68 on dark background, red progress bar fills to 68%, 'WITHIN 18 MONTHS' badge springs in

narration: Banks don't raise limits randomly. They analyze your spending history, find your sweet spot, and time the offer perfectly — it's science, not generosity.
visual: Bank building SVG (columns, triangle roof) on light background, arrow pointing from bank to credit card, 'NOT RANDOM. CALCULATED.' text fades in

narration: The average American takes on $2,400 in new debt after a limit increase. At 24% APR, that free upgrade costs you $576 a year just in interest.
visual: $2,400 debt counter animating on dark background, growing red bar chart columns, '$576/YR IN INTEREST' badge reveals at the end

narration: Here's your counter-move: call your bank and ask them to lower your limit. Remove the ceiling, remove the trap — your future self wins.
visual: Phone SVG on light background, green checkmark springs in beside it, 'CALL YOUR BANK. REQUEST LOWER LIMIT.' text, dark CTA card 'REMOVE THE CEILING / REMOVE THE TRAP'
